home.page_title: Department of Obstetric and Gynaecological Nursing - Faculty of Nursing, Mahidol University
home.title:
Department of Obstetric and
Gynaecological Nursing
home.subtitle: Department of Obstetric and Gynaecological Nursing is a professional leader in academy, research, and service of midwifery and maternal-newborn and families, including women’s health. The Department is also responsible for all academic endeavors leading to the excellence in the care of maternity, newborn, and midwifery.
home.academic_desc: Provides undergraduate and graduate education with modern teaching styles focusing on student-centered learning to produce quality and moral nursing graduates.
home.research_desc: Promotes research to generate knowledge and innovation in obstetric and gynaecological nursing and midwifery.
home.service_desc: Integrates midwifery and obstetric-gynaecological nursing knowledge to promote the health of women, children, and families.

about.page_title: About us | Department of Obstetric and Gynaecological Nursing
about.title: About us
about.intro_p1: Department of Obstetric and Gynaecological Nursing is an academic leader in education, research, and academic service related to midwifery, maternal-newborn and family nursing, as well as women's health nursing, to improve the quality of life of women, mothers, newborns, and families, which yields long-term benefits to society.
about.intro_p2: The department has a total of 29 personnel, consisting of 28 faculty members and 1 administrative support staff.
about.rank_title: Classified by academic rank, there are:
about.role_director: Director of the School of Midwifery and Nursing
about.history_p1: Following Her Majesty Queen Sri Bajrindra, the Queen Consort of King Chulalongkorn (Rama V), who had experienced both traditional childbirth and modern midwifery when she gave birth to His Royal Highness Prince Asdang Dejavudh on May 17, 1888. Under the care of Dr. Peter Gowan in the modern midwifery style, Her Majesty discontinued the traditional postpartum practice of lying by the fire (Yu Fai) and stated that not lying by the fire was much more comfortable. She then encouraged her royal court to receive modern midwifery services, which gained widespread popularity. Her Majesty recognized that midwifery in Thailand lacked properly qualified and knowledgeable modern midwives, as pregnant women were still cared for in the traditional way, leading to high risks and dangers. Consequently, she donated her personal funds to establish the School of Midwifery and Nursing at Siriraj Hospital. The school officially opened on January 12, 1896, to provide education for women in midwifery and nursing.
about.history_p2: When the School of Midwifery and Nursing was established for the first time in Thailand, it marked the birth of midwifery and maternal-newborn nursing education alongside the nursing school itself, which later evolved into the present-day Faculty of Nursing, Mahidol University. The Department of Obstetric and Gynaecological Nursing has progressed and evolved hand-in-hand with the School of Midwifery and Nursing, making it the first and oldest nursing department in Thailand. The administration of midwifery and maternal-infant care education has continuously adapted and revised its curriculum in response to societal and scientific advancements. The department was officially upgraded from a division to the Department of Obstetric and Gynaecological Nursing on February 18, 1974, by the organizing committee of the Faculty of Nursing, chaired by Mrs. Sanguansuk Chantawong, Director of Siriraj School of Nursing, Midwifery, and Public Health at that time. This official approval was published in the Special Royal Gazette, Volume 91, Section 37, pages 49-50, dated February 28, 1974 (B.E. 2517).

vision.page_title: Vision & Mission | Department of Obstetric and Gynaecological Nursing
vision.title: Mission & Vision
vision.vision_desc: The Department of Obstetric and Gynaecological Nursing is a leader in midwifery and women's health education and research in Asia.
vision.mission_desc: The Department of Obstetric and Gynaecological Nursing is committed to providing excellent and socially responsive midwifery and obstetric-gynaecological nursing education to undergraduate and graduate nursing students. The department continuously supports and develops faculty potential in academic instruction, research, and textbook writing. It delivers high-quality academic services to improve the quality of life of target populations and the general public, while fostering cultural awareness and preserving Thai arts and heritage.
vision.philosophy_desc: We believe that maternal-child care, women's health, and midwifery services are crucial foundations for strong families and a healthy nation.
vision.strategic_desc: To achieve excellence in nursing education, enhance international research collaborations, and build strong community nursing clinics.
vision.pillar1_desc: Provides undergraduate and graduate nursing education, aiming for academic excellence and addressing social needs.
vision.pillar2_desc: Promotes continuous faculty research potential and the authorship of academic textbooks in obstetric-gynaecological nursing.
vision.pillar3_desc: Improves and promotes the quality of life for target groups and the general public through standard nursing services.
vision.pillar4_desc: Promotes and develops a sense of responsibility for the preservation and conservation of valuable Thai arts and culture.

research.page_title: Research | Department of Obstetric and Gynaecological Nursing
research.title: Research
research.intro: The Department of Obstetric and Gynaecological Nursing aims to promote and support faculty members in producing high-quality research. This research builds new knowledge and nursing innovations that benefit the profession and society, with a particular focus on sustainably improving healthcare quality for women, infants, and families.
research.proj1: A study of health behaviors in pregnant women

academic.page_title: Academic Services | Department of Obstetric and Gynaecological Nursing
academic.title: Academic Services
academic.intro: The Department of Obstetric and Gynaecological Nursing has a policy to support faculty members in providing academic services concerning the health promotion of women, pregnant women, intrapartum and postpartum mothers, and newborns, to healthcare professionals and the general public. The department currently manages the following academic service projects:
academic.project_laos: Capacity Building Project for Nurses in Caring for Pregnant, Intrapartum, and Postpartum Women (Lao PDR)
academic.project_teenager: Continuous Care System Development Project for Pregnant Adolescents by Primary Midwives
academic.project_sex_education: Sexual Health and Sex Education Awareness Project for Female Adolescents and Students
academic.project_diabetes: "Health Promotion for Women with Gestational Diabetes Integrated with Instruction" Project

congrat.title: Congratulations | Department of Obstetric and Gynaecological Nursing, Faculty of Nursing, Mahidol University
congrat.c1_title: Congratulations to Assistant Professor Dr. Nareerat Jitramontree
congrat.c1_desc: Received a research grant from Mahidol University for the project "Establishment of an Integrated Elderly Well-being and Palliative Care Learning Center" under the integrated research program for preventing elderly falls in the community, amounting to 351,000 THB.
congrat.c2_title: Congratulations to Assistant Professor Natsurang Boonchan
congrat.c2_desc: Received a research grant from Mahidol University for the project "Establishment of an Integrated Elderly Well-being and Palliative Care Learning Center: A case study of Nong Phlab Subdistrict, Hua Hin District, Prachuap Khiri Khan Province," amounting to 808,000 THB.
congrat.c3_title: Congratulations to Assistant Professor Natsurang Boonchan
congrat.c3_desc: Received a research grant from CMB for the project "Predictors of health beliefs on preventive behaviors for peripheral arterial occlusive disease in high-risk elderly populations," amounting to 80,000 THB.
congrat.c4_title: Congratulations to Assistant Professor Dr. Wiraporn Wirojratana
congrat.c4_desc: Received a research grant from National Research Council of Thailand (NRCT) for the project "Cost-effectiveness analysis of a nurse-led self-control program in care of elderly with Type 2 Diabetes in communities," amounting to 809,466 THB.
congrat.c5_title: Congratulations to Associate Professor Dr. Wilaiwan Thongcharoen
congrat.c5_desc: Received a research grant from Mahidol University for the project "Establishment of an Integrated Elderly Well-being and Palliative Care Learning Center: A palliative care model for elderly at the end of life," amounting to 582,000 THB.
congrat.c6_title: Congratulations to Associate Professor Venus Leelahakul
congrat.c6_desc: Received a research grant from Thai Health Promotion Foundation (ThaiHealth) for the project "Nutritional promotion for chronic disease control in elderly populations," amounting to 150,000 THB.
congrat.c7_title: Congratulations to Associate Professor Prangtip Ujarattan
congrat.c7_desc: Received a research grant from CMB for the project "Learning outcomes of nursing students in lecture-based versus team-based learning," amounting to 35,200 THB.
congrat.c8_title: Congratulations to Assistant Professor Dr. Saowalak Jirathamkhun
congrat.c8_desc: Received a research grant from Ministry of Public Health and WHO for the project "The emerging roles of private nursing schools in Thailand," amounting to 500,000 THB.
